title: thebasics-handbook-title-rp-chat
text: RP chat turns normal chat into proximity-based roleplay text when enabled. Ranges, formatting, bubbles, and many commands are server-configurable. Speech modes - /say [message], aliases /s and /normal: speak normally or set your default mode to normal. - /whisper [message], alias /w: whisper once or set whisper as your default mode. - /yell [message], alias /y: yell once or set yell as your default mode. Default ranges are usually whisper 5 blocks, normal 35 blocks, and yell 90 blocks. Emotes and environment text - /me <emote>: proximity emote. - /it <message> or /do <message>: nearby environmental description. - /envhere <message>: place environment text where you are looking; if no surface is hit, it falls back above you. - /emotemode [on|off]: make ordinary proximity messages act like emotes unless another syntax says otherwise. Typing shortcuts These defaults can be changed by server config: *text for emote, !text for environment, !!text for placed environment, (text) for local OOC, and ((text)) for global OOC when enabled. OOC - /ooc <message>: local out-of-character message. - /ooctoggle [on|off]: toggle OOC chat mode when allowed. - /gooc <message>: global OOC when enabled. Presentation controls - /rptext [on|off]: toggle whether your proximity chat goes through The BASICs RP pipeline. - /chatter [on|off]: toggle hearing character chatter sounds when the server has chatter enabled. Sign language is silent; speech and emotes with quoted speech can produce chatter. - Overhead bubbles can be RP-styled, vanilla-style, or disabled by server config. Typing indicators are also config-driven.
